name: GitHub CLI Installer
description: Install the GitHub CLI (gh). Two strategies controlled by 'method': 'repos' (default) adds and uses the official GitHub CLI package repository on Debian/Ubuntu (apt) and RHEL/Fedora/SUSE (rpm/dnf), the native community package on Alpine (apk) and Arch (pacman), and Homebrew on macOS; 'binary' downloads the pre-built binary from GitHub Releases for all platforms including Alpine (the Linux binaries are statically linked with CGO_ENABLED=0). Version is controlled by the 'version' option.

version.description:
Version of gh to install.
'latest' (default) — with method=repos: install the latest version from the configured repository. With method=binary: query the GitHub Releases API for the newest release.
A version string (e.g. '2.89.0') — with method=repos: on apt, passes 'gh=<version>' to apt-get. On macOS (Homebrew), Alpine, Arch, and RHEL-based systems (dnf/yum/zypper), version pinning is not supported: a warning is logged and the latest available package is installed instead. With method=binary: downloads exactly that release from github.com/cli/cli/releases.

method.description:
Installation strategy.
'repos' (default) — configure and use the official GitHub CLI package repository. On Debian/Ubuntu: adds the official apt repo (cli.github.com/packages) with GPG keyring and installs via apt. On RHEL/Fedora/CentOS/Amazon Linux: adds the official rpm repo and installs via dnf/yum. On SUSE/openSUSE: adds the official rpm repo and installs via zypper. On Alpine: installs via 'apk add github-cli' (community package, no version pinning). On Arch: installs via 'pacman -S github-cli' (community package, no version pinning). On macOS: installs via Homebrew ('brew install gh').
'binary' — download the pre-built binary archive from github.com/cli/cli/releases; verify SHA-256 against the release checksums file; install the 'gh' binary to $prefix/bin. The Linux release binaries are statically linked (CGO_ENABLED=0), so this method works on all Linux distributions including Alpine/musl without any compatibility shim. shell_completions controls which shells receive completion scripts.

prefix.description:
Installation prefix when method=binary. The 'gh' binary is placed at '$prefix/bin/gh'.
'auto' (default) — resolves to /usr/local when running as root, or $HOME/.local when running as non-root.
An explicit path — used as-is; the script exits with an error if the path is not writable and cannot be created.
Ignored when method=repos (the package manager controls the install location, typically /usr/bin).

symlink.description:
Create a symlink from the canonical bin directory to $prefix/bin/gh when prefix resolves to a non-default path (method=binary only).
The symlink location is determined by whether $prefix falls under any user's home directory (as listed in /etc/passwd):
  System-wide (prefix is not under any user home): creates /usr/local/bin/gh -> $prefix/bin/gh.
  User-scoped  (prefix is under a user home):        creates $HOME/.local/bin/gh -> $prefix/bin/gh.
Ensures the containerEnv PATH entries always resolve to the installed binary regardless of the chosen prefix.
No-op when $prefix/bin/gh is already the chosen target path, or when method=repos (gh lands in /usr/bin which is always on PATH).

shell_completions.description:
Space-separated list of shell names to install completions for.
Supported shells: 'bash', 'zsh'.
With method=binary: completion files are taken from the release archive.
With method=repos: completions are generated by running 'gh completion -s <shell>' after installation.
As root: writes bash completion to '/etc/bash_completion.d/gh' and zsh completion to '<zshdir>/completions/_gh' (where <zshdir> is detected by shell__detect_zshdir).
As non-root: writes to '$HOME/.local/share/bash-completion/completions/gh' (bash) and '$HOME/.zfunc/_gh' (zsh).
Set to '' to skip all completion writes.
Examples: 'bash zsh', 'bash', 'zsh', ''.

if_exists.description:
What to do when 'gh' is already present in PATH before installation begins.
If the installed version matches the resolved target version, installation is always skipped silently regardless of this setting.
'skip' (default) — log a notice and exit successfully without making any changes.
'fail' — print an error and exit non-zero.

extensions.description: Comma-separated list of GitHub CLI extensions to install after gh itself is installed. Each entry is passed verbatim to 'gh extension install', so any argument that command accepts is valid: a GitHub repository slug ('owner/repo'), a full https:// URL, or a local directory path. Example: 'dlvhdr/gh-dash,github/gh-copilot'. Extensions are installed for the users resolved by the add_*_user_config and add_user_config options. Set to '' to skip extension installation.

add_current_user_config.description:
Apply per-user git configuration (git_protocol, setup_git, sign_commits) and install extensions for the current non-root user (SUDO_USER if run via sudo, otherwise the result of whoami).
No effect when the current user is root and other non-root users are also being targeted.
Only applies when at least one of 'extensions', 'git_protocol', 'setup_git', or 'sign_commits' is active.

add_remote_user_config.description:
Apply per-user git configuration (git_protocol, setup_git, sign_commits) and install extensions for the devcontainer remoteUser (the _REMOTE_USER environment variable set by the devcontainer tooling).
No effect when _REMOTE_USER is not set.
Only applies when at least one of 'extensions', 'git_protocol', 'setup_git', or 'sign_commits' is active.

add_container_user_config.description:
Apply per-user git configuration (git_protocol, setup_git, sign_commits) and install extensions for the devcontainer containerUser (the _CONTAINER_USER environment variable set by the devcontainer tooling).
No effect when _CONTAINER_USER is not set.
Only applies when at least one of 'extensions', 'git_protocol', 'setup_git', or 'sign_commits' is active.

add_user_config.description: Comma-separated list of additional usernames to apply per-user git configuration and install extensions for (e.g. 'alice,bob'). Each user must already exist. Root is accepted here if explicitly listed. Only applies when at least one of 'extensions', 'git_protocol', 'setup_git', or 'sign_commits' is active.

git_protocol.description:
Set the default git protocol in gh's per-user config (~/.config/gh/config.yml) by running 'gh config set git_protocol <value>' for each resolved user.
'https' — use HTTPS for git clone and push operations (gh's built-in default).
'ssh' — use SSH for git clone and push.
'' (empty, default) — leave the config key unset; gh uses its own default (https).
Applied for all users resolved by the add_*_user_config / add_user_config options.

setup_git.description:
Register gh as the git credential helper by running 'gh auth setup-git --force --hostname <git_hostname>' for each resolved user.
This writes a credential helper entry to ~/.gitconfig, allowing git operations to authenticate via gh without a separate credential store.
'--force' is used so the command succeeds at container build time even when there is no active gh login yet.
Defaulting to false to avoid unexpected changes to user gitconfig; set to true in devcontainer environments where gh will be the primary authentication mechanism.

sign_commits.description:
Pre-configure commit signing in each resolved user's ~/.gitconfig.
'' (default) — skip; make no changes to commit signing config.
'ssh' — set gpg.format=ssh and commit.gpgsign=true. Requires git 2.34+. Well-suited for devcontainers with SSH agent forwarding (via runArgs/remoteEnv in devcontainer.json): signing is silent and needs no TTY or pinentry. Users must still set user.signingkey themselves (e.g. via dotfiles: 'git config --global user.signingkey ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub'). For commits to show as Verified on GitHub, the corresponding public key must be registered under GitHub Settings > SSH and GPG keys > New SSH signing key.
'gpg' — set commit.gpgsign=true and explicitly unset gpg.format so git uses its default GPG format. Suited for users with an existing GPG key and gpg-agent already configured. Users must set user.signingkey to their GPG key ID (e.g. 'git config --global user.signingkey 3AA5C34371567BD2'). Note: gpg-agent socket forwarding into containers requires additional host setup beyond standard devcontainer spec; SSH signing is simpler in container environments.
In both cases, 'user.signingkey' is intentionally not set by this feature because the key identifier is user-specific and unknown at build time.

git_hostname.description:
The hostname to configure as the git credential helper target when setup_git=true.
Defaults to 'github.com'. Set to your GitHub Enterprise Server hostname (e.g. 'git.corp.example.com') when using GHES.
Ignored when setup_git=false.

installer_dir.description:
Working directory used for method=binary: the binary archive and checksums file are downloaded and extracted here.
Removed automatically after a successful install unless keep_installer=true.
Ignored when method=repos.

keep_installer.description:
Keep the installer_dir after a successful install when method=binary.
Useful for debugging or inspecting the extracted archive.
Ignored when method=repos.

logfile.description: Append install log to this file path.
